strategy_notes: Funnel + content-type strategy. 55% practical (how-to, case study, tool comparison) / 45% news. 70% business / 30% finance. Targeting CEO, CFO, COO. Marketing Director may propose max 2 field changes per run with rationale.

change_log.change: Added content_type (practical/news) to schedule. Reweighted 70% business / 30% finance. Version 2.1→3.0.

practical.name: Practical — How-tos, Case Studies, Tool Comparisons
practical.description: Actionable content for executives implementing AI. Step-by-step guides, vendor comparisons, real-world case studies with measurable outcomes.
examples: How Accountants Can Boost Productivity 40% Using Claude in Excel
Enterprise AI Copilots Compared: Microsoft 365 Copilot vs Google Duet AI for CFOs
How JPMorgan Deployed AI Fraud Detection — Timeline, Cost, and Results

news.name: News — Analysis & Implications
news.description: Current events analysis focused on business implications. Every news article must answer: what should the reader DO with this information?

business.audience: CEO, COO, operations leaders implementing AI

news_analysis.name: News Analysis
news_analysis.description: Breaking AI news with business implications. Must answer: what should the reader DO?

deep_dive.name: Deep Dive
deep_dive.description: In-depth analysis of a trend, study, or strategic shift. Data-heavy, evaluative.

case_study.name: Case Study
case_study.description: Real-world AI deployment with timeline, costs, results, lessons learned.

how_to.name: How-To Guide
how_to.description: Step-by-step implementation guide with prerequisites, failure scenarios, success metrics.

technology_profile.name: Technology Profile
technology_profile.description: Vendor/tool comparison or technology landscape assessment with scoring criteria.

industry_briefing.name: Industry Briefing
industry_briefing.description: Sector-specific AI adoption roundup. Rotating: Logistics, Manufacturing, Healthcare, Professional Services, Retail, Energy.

TOF.name: Top of Funnel — Awareness & Myth-Busting
TOF.purpose: Challenge AI myths, build traffic via searchable questions, drive readers toward MOF content.
TOF.audience: Early-stage decision-maker, scanning, skeptical. 3-5 min read.
TOF.h1_format: Question-focused: 'Does X?', 'Can X?', 'Should X?', 'Is X really?'
mandatory_sections: H1 (question-focused, searchable)
The Most Common Misconception (the myth being challenged)
What Research Actually Shows (findings in plain language)
Where This Narrative Breaks (1-2 real scenarios where myth fails)
What You Should Actually Do (clear, actionable recommendation)
Clear Verdict (Believe the hype? Ignore it? When it matters?)

anchor_text_examples: Read the full research breakdown on [topic]
See how this plays out in real implementations

forbidden: Detailed study methodology sections (too technical for awareness stage)
TOF.tone: Conversational, direct verdict, myth-busting energy. Simple answer approach.

MOF.name: Middle of Funnel — Deep Analysis & Decision Support
MOF.purpose: Translate research for decision-makers, reduce decision risk, establish authority through nuanced analysis.
MOF.audience: Director/VP with budget authority, 10-15 min read, evaluating $500K+ decision.
MOF.h1_format: Claim or decision-focused: 'Why X fails in real companies', 'What research shows about X (and where it fails)'
MOF.seo_intent: Long-tail decision keywords: ROI, implementation, risks, benefits
mandatory_sections: H1 (claim or question, specific, decision-focused)
What the Study Actually Tested (research question, sample, timeframe, key limitations)
What the Results Show (main finding + business translation, including negatives)
Why These Results Are Often Misused (named misuse patterns, challenge popular narratives)
What the Study Does NOT Prove (3-5 explicit non-claims with explanations)
Where This Breaks in Real Organizations (3-5 specific friction scenarios)
What This Means for [Specific Business Functions] (2-3 functions: applicability, changes, risks)
Clear Judgment (when works, when doesn't, why cautious — no hedging)

anchor_text_examples: How to implement [topic] without the common failures

forbidden: Hedged verdicts ('it depends' without specific conditions)
MOF.tone: Expert, balanced, credible. Challenges narratives. Explicit conclusions. No hedging.

BOF.name: Bottom of Funnel — Implementation & Action Guide
BOF.purpose: Reduce implementation risk, enable success, capture ready-to-decide audience.
BOF.audience: Project manager or C-suite leading implementation, 15-20 min read, already committed to investing.
BOF.h1_format: Action-focused: 'How to implement X without Y', '7 implementation risks for X', 'Step-by-step guide to X'
mandatory_sections: H1 (implementation-focused, not theoretical)
What You Need to Be True First (3-5 preconditions with explanations)
Step-by-Step Implementation (5-7 numbered steps: what-to-do, why it matters, watch-for)
Where This Fails (3-5 specific failure scenarios from real implementations)
Success Metrics (primary metric + 2-3 secondary, leading vs lagging indicators)
Decision Checkpoint (3-4 explicit go/no-go criteria before full rollout)
Clear Verdict (Proceed / Proceed cautiously / Wait — explicit, not hedged)
forbidden: Vague verdicts like 'it depends' without conditions
BOF.tone: Practical, action-oriented, risk-aware. 'Here is how to do this right' energy.

internal_linking_strategy.goal: Drive visitors progressively down the funnel: TOF (awareness) → MOF (evaluation) → BOF (implementation). Each stage pulls the reader toward conversion and ad engagement.
internal_linking_strategy.principle: TOF articles send readers deeper. MOF articles bridge awareness and action. BOF articles close with confidence.
internal_linking_strategy.link_format: Use descriptive anchor text. Never 'click here' or 'read more'. Use topic-specific text: 'our analysis of AI productivity ROI' not 'read more here'.
internal_linking_strategy.recent_post_linking: Writer receives the 10 most recent post titles and slugs. Link to them when topically relevant using relative Hugo paths: /posts/slug-here/

ai_tells_to_avoid: em dash overuse — used as stylistic connector more than once per paragraph is a strong AI signal
at the end of the day
the fact of the matter is

evolution_rules.rollback_rule: If metric_to_watch worsens by more than 10% after 14 days, revert the change and log it.
